hypotheses.cause: Config rollout breaks requests AND wedges queue consumers
hypotheses.reasoning_summary: The logs explicitly show work_failed errors due to an invalid_feature_mode (turbo_v2), which aligns with a bad configuration rollout. Concurrently, the queue_depth remains stuck at 11 and jobs_processed is not advancing, indicating the queue consumers are wedged by the invalid configuration and failing to process backlog, while user-facing /work requests are returning 500 errors.

hypotheses.cause: Invalid feature flag configuration causing 500 errors on /work endpoint
hypotheses.reasoning_summary: The application is rejecting requests to the /work endpoint with 500 Internal Server Error due to an unrecognized feature_mode 'turbo_v2'. This indicates a misconfiguration or unsupported feature flag being passed to the application, causing all work requests to fail, though this hypothesis does not fully account for the wedged queue consumers.

consulted_runbooks:
Queue backlog growing while consumers sit idle (score=0.1066): Symptoms: queue_depth grows monotonically; jobs_processed counter stops advancing; user-facing requests and health checks stay green — a silent backlog with no error logs.
Diagnosis: compare producer and consumer rates over a window; check the worker/consumer process state (paused, crashed, deadlocked); verify the queue itself accepts reads.
Remediation: restart (or unpause) the worker service so

Queue consumer scaled to zero replicas (score=0.0907): Symptoms: worker_shutdown (SIGTERM) in the logs and then silence; queue_depth climbing while jobs_processed stays flat; health checks green because nothing user-facing is failing yet.
Remediation: scale the consumer back to its baseline replica count. IMPORTANT: restarting the service is a NO-OP when zero replicas exist — there is no container to restart; an explicit scale-up is required. Treat th

Config rollout breaks requests AND wedges queue consumers (score=0.0767): Symptoms: work_failed errors naming an invalid setting (e.g. invalid_feature_mode) right after a rollout, while queue_depth sits above zero without draining and jobs_processed stops advancing.
Remediation: roll the configuration back AND restart the consumer as well — a consumer that loaded the bad config stays wedged after the rollback, so the backlog will not drain on its own. Verify both the er

proposal.hypothesis_cause: Config rollout breaks requests AND wedges queue consumers

steps.expected_effect: Restores canonical app config, removing the invalid feature_mode causing request failures.
Restarts the wedged worker consumer so it loads the rolled-back config and resumes draining the queue backlog.

rollback_plan.expected_effect: Re-applies the invalid configuration to restore the pre-remediation app state.
Restarts the worker to load the re-applied invalid configuration, restoring the pre-remediation wedged state.
